title: Vapor Pressure and Clausius-Clapeyron Equation
content: The vapor pressure P of a pure liquid increases with temperature T according to the Clausius-Clapeyron equation: d(ln P)/dT = ΔHvap/(RT²), or in integrated form ln(P2/P1) = −ΔHvap/R · (1/T2 − 1/T1). Plotting ln P vs 1/T yields a straight line with slope −ΔHvap/R, enabling enthalpy of vaporization to be determined experimentally at low cost.
